label: Topics
description: Controls which CS and programming concepts appear in generated problems.

fields.label: Practice topics
fields.description: The CS and programming concepts you want to practice. Generated problems will draw from this list.

fields.label: Topics to avoid
fields.description: Concepts you want excluded from generated problems. Any topic here is also removed from the include list automatically.

label: Difficulty
description: Sets the difficulty range for generated problems across three independent dimensions, each rated 1-5.

fields.label: Algorithm complexity range
fields.description: Complexity of the algorithm required. Level 1 is simple iteration. Level 2 is binary search or hash map lookup. Level 3 is BFS/DFS or basic DP. Level 4 is Dijkstra's or 2D DP. Level 5 is advanced algorithms like network flow.

fields.label: Data structure complexity range
fields.description: Complexity of data structures involved. Level 1 is arrays and strings only. Level 2 adds hash maps, stacks, queues. Level 3 adds trees, heaps, graphs. Level 4 requires custom or augmented structures. Level 5 is tries, segment trees, or similar.

fields.label: Problem complexity range
fields.description: Difficulty of understanding and decomposing the problem. Level 1 is obvious what to do. Level 2 needs one insight. Level 3 needs pattern recognition. Level 4 needs chaining multiple insights. Level 5 requires reframing the problem entirely.

label: Style
description: Controls the framing style of generated problems.

fields.label: Style preference
fields.description: How problems are framed. LeetCode style uses abstract function signatures with numerical constraints. Real-world style frames problems in a meaningful domain context like scheduling or inventory management.

options.label: LeetCode only
options.description: Abstract algorithmic problems with function signatures and numerical constraints.

options.label: Real-world only
options.description: Problems framed in practical domain contexts like scheduling, caching, or data pipelines.

options.label: Mixed
options.description: Both LeetCode and real-world styles appear, selected based on what fits the concept.

fields.label: Allowed styles
fields.description: Which styles the generator is allowed to use. Typically matches the preference but can restrict a mixed preference to specific styles.

label: Language
description: Controls which programming languages have test suites generated.

fields.label: Language preference
fields.description: Which language(s) to generate test suites for. Choosing both creates test files for JavaScript and Python.

options.label: Both
options.description: Generate test suites for both JavaScript and Python.

label: Parts
description: Controls how many parts generated problems have. Parts build on each other progressively.

fields.label: Part count range
fields.description: The minimum and maximum number of parts a generated problem can have. More parts mean longer, multi-step problems.

fields.label: Maximum parts (hard ceiling)
fields.description: Absolute maximum number of parts any generated problem can have. This ceiling is never exceeded regardless of other settings.

label: Surprise Me
description: Controls whether generation parameters are selected randomly or asked interactively.

fields.label: Enable Surprise Me
fields.description: When enabled, topics, style, difficulty, and part count are selected randomly from your configured ranges. When disabled, the agent asks you for each parameter interactively.

label: Hide Problem Details
description: Controls what information is visible during and after problem generation. Prevents seeing problem concepts, structure, or file contents before you start solving.

fields.label: Enable detail hiding
fields.description: Master switch for all detail hiding. When disabled, all hide flags below are ignored and full generation details are shown.

fields.label: Hide topics
fields.description: Prevents the agent from revealing which topics the problem covers during generation. You discover the topic by reading the problem description.

fields.label: Hide style
fields.description: Prevents the agent from revealing whether the problem is LeetCode or real-world style. You discover the style from the problem framing.

fields.label: Hide part count
fields.description: Prevents the agent from revealing how many parts the problem has. You discover parts progressively as you solve.

fields.label: Hide file output
fields.description: Routes all file writes through a background script so problem.json and test suite contents are not displayed in the terminal during generation. Prevents accidentally seeing test cases or problem structure.

label: Time Range
description: Sets the range of expected problem-solving times in minutes.

fields.label: Expected time range (minutes)
fields.description: The minimum and maximum expected solving time for generated problems. Problems are assigned a time within this range based on their difficulty.
